title: HTTP Strict Transport Security
description: HTTP header that forces browsers to always use HTTPS.
fullExplanation: HSTS is an HTTP response header that tells browsers: 'Always use HTTPS for this domain, never HTTP'. Once a browser sees the HSTS header, it will automatically upgrade all HTTP requests to HTTPS for the specified duration (max-age). This prevents SSL stripping attacks. Even if a user types http://example.com, the browser will internally change it to https://example.com before sending the request.

directives.name: max-age
directives.description: How long (in seconds) browser should remember to use HTTPS

directives.name: includeSubDomains
directives.description: Apply HSTS to all subdomains (www.example.com, api.example.com, etc.)

directives.name: preload
directives.description: Request inclusion in browser's HSTS preload list

howItWorks.description: Browser stores HSTS policy for 1 year
User types http://example.com → browser auto-upgrades to https://
Upgrade happens before any network traffic (prevents MITM)

keyFacts: Browser automatically upgrades HTTP to HTTPS
First visit must be HTTPS (trust on first use problem)
HSTS Preload list solves first-visit problem
Cannot be disabled by user (until max-age expires)
Invalid certificates cause hard failure (no bypass option)

preloadList.description: Browsers ship with hardcoded list of HSTS domains
preloadList.benefit: Protects even on first visit (no trust on first use)
requirements: Serve HSTS header on all subdomains

limitations: First visit must be HTTPS (unless preloaded)
Cannot easily remove from preload list
Breaks sites that need HTTP for some subdomains
